sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Chief Complaint
Neck pain following a motor vehicle collision.
History of Present Illness
The patient reports neck pain following a car accident approximately one week prior. He describes the injury as a forward head movement with rapid acceleration, resulting in pain. The patient rates the pain as 7/10 and notes it is not constant, but worsens with head movement. Ibuprofen provided mild relief. He reports occasional headaches related to the neck pain. He has a history of playing football years ago but denies any prior neck injuries. He did not seek emergency room care after the accident due to cost concerns.
Findings
Physical examination reveals tenderness to palpation of the cervical muscles, positive findings on range of motion testing including pain with flexion, extension, and rotation. No swelling or bruising is noted on physical examination. Neurological examination reveals intact sensation and motor function.
Assessment
The patient's symptoms and examination findings are consistent with a cervical strain secondary to whiplash.
Plan

sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value: I am prescribing motrin 600 mg every 6 to 8 hours as needed for pain relief. I am also prescribing flexeril 10 mg every 12 hours as needed for muscle relaxation. I have ordered an MRI to rule out concussion. I am referring the patient to physical rehabilitation for evaluation and treatment. Follow up in four weeks or sooner if symptoms worsen.
